summary:
The Bennett angle measures the deviation from the sagittal plane formed by the working or non-working condylar path during lateral jaw movements. It specifically relates to how the condyle moves when one side of the mouth opens laterally, reflecting normal joint mechanics under functional conditions. 

Note: Do NOT include any commentary about correctness or accuracy beyond stating facts based solely upon provided information; avoid adding external knowledge such as "this is commonly seen" etc., unless explicitly stated within input text.

Bennett angle represents the angle between the resting sagittal plane passing through the condyles and the trajectory traced by the non-working condyle during lateral excursive movements. Clinically it quantifies the directionality of condylar displacement observed in unilateral opening motions, providing insight into temporomandibular joint biomechanics.
